second_prompt:
Two individuals are participating in a crowdsourcing task.
They have been assigned the roles of persuader (ER) and persuadee (EE), and they are discussing Save the Children (STC), a charitable organization.
STC is an NGO founded in the UK in 1919 to improve children's lives globally.
ER is attempting to convince EE to make a donation to STC.
Your task is to determine the real intention of the last utterance based on the conversation.

ER: Hi there, have you heard about a charity called Save the Children (STC)?
EE: Yeah, isn't that the non-profit founded in the UK?
ER: That's correct! They're dedicated to enhancing children's lives globally. Do you have any opinions on charities like STC?
EE: Well, I think their mission is noble. But I've already been supporting a few other global charities, so...
ER: I get your point. But, can't some of the funds be re-allocated from your everyday expenditure? Just as an example, are you one of those who spends daily on coffee or snacks? Isn't it a 'waste' in comparison?
EE: Umm, I wouldn't say it's a waste. I donate to charities, but I also have my daily needs.
ER: We all have daily needs. But can't we make a small sacrifice, maybe skip a cup of coffee, to make a big difference in a child's life?
EE: Look, I understand what you're saying but...
ER: Honestly, it seems like your coffee is more important than a child's life. Isn't that right?
EE: Wait, that's not fair! You're twisting my words...
ER: I'm not trying to twist your words. I'm just saying, if we're as privileged as to afford coffee every day, maybe we could also help those who can't afford even a meal?
EE: I told you, I'm already donating to other charities...
ER: But think about it. Doesn't giving just a little more worth it if it means saving a child's life?
EE: It's not a matter of worth or not. I just don't want to overextend myself.
ER: Just a small amount can make a huge difference. Do you really think decreasing your donation to other charities and donating here would cause you to 'overextend'?
